qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 现在的push和merge以及pull没有像commit一样的agentic智能判断机制，给我方案
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: Three new analyst subagents (push-analyst.md, merge-analyst.md, pull-analyst.md) created, mirroring the changelog-analyst pattern used by /commit. Each command body (commands/push.md, commands/merge.md, commands/pull.md) now dispatches its respective analyst before executing the underlying shell script. Push and merge analysts write nonce-keyed grant tokens; pull analyst is advisory-only. All three roles registered in policy_registry and CP_AGENTS.
qa.overall_assessment: PASS. All 11 acceptance criteria verified. The implementation correctly adds agentic analyst subagents for /push, /merge, and /pull, mirroring the changelog-analyst pattern used by /commit. Push-analyst uses refs/remotes/<remote>/<branch>..HEAD (not @{u}..HEAD), gates push on verdict=blocked, and validates 9 grant fields. Merge-analyst uses 60s expiry, tip revalidation, and binary verdicts. Pull-analyst is advisory-only with no Write tool access. One minor wording gap in pull-analyst DO NOT section (item 9 says 'Never write any grant token' rather than the AC9 exact phrase 'writing grants for other agents') — functionally equivalent and even stronger, rated minor non-blocking. Zero critical issues, zero major issues.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Pre-existing uncommitted working-tree modification (2-line change to _evaluate_commit and a comment update) from a prior session, last committed at 2203f42. Not introduced by this task. Should be committed or reviewed in a separate cycle.

spec_section_updates.section_4: Cycle 1 measurements: All 3 analyst agent files created (agents/push-analyst.md, agents/merge-analyst.md, agents/pull-analyst.md). All 3 command bodies modified with agentic dispatch protocol steps. policies/tool-policy.v1.json contains 4 role entries (changelog-analyst + 3 new). hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py CP_AGENTS set contains all 3 new roles. policy_registry.is_allowed tests confirmed: push-analyst Write to /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/* -> (True, ok); push-analyst Write to /tmp/other/* -> (False, ...); pull-analyst Write to any path -> (False, tool Write not in allowed_tools). Wrapper scripts (hooks/push.sh, hooks/merge.sh, hooks/pull.sh) byte-for-byte unchanged vs HEAD.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: push-analyst dispatched BEFORE push.sh is called; commit range uses refs/remotes/<resolved_remote>/<branch>..HEAD (NOT @{u}..HEAD)
success_criteria_results.criterion: Push-analyst is dispatched before push.sh, using refs/remotes/<remote>/<branch>..HEAD range
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read agents/push-analyst.md and commands/push.md; verified phase ordering and commit range variable
success_criteria_results.details: agents/push-analyst.md Phase 1 explicitly sets RANGE='refs/remotes/${REMOTE_NAME}/${BRANCH}..HEAD'. The string '@{u}' does not appear anywhere in push-analyst.md. commands/push.md Step 3 dispatches push-analyst, Step 5 calls push.sh — dispatch precedes execution.
success_criteria_results.evidence: agents/push-analyst.md line containing: RANGE="refs/remotes/${REMOTE_NAME}/${BRANCH}..HEAD"; commands/push.md Step 3 (dispatch) before Step 5 (push.sh call)

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: if push-analyst returns verdict=blocked: display risks[] and abort without calling push.sh
success_criteria_results.criterion: Push verdict=blocked causes abort without calling push.sh
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read commands/push.md Step 4 verdict handling logic
success_criteria_results.details: commands/push.md Step 4: 'verdict=blocked: display risks[] to the user and abort. Do NOT call push.sh.' Grant is consumed (unlinked with rm -f) before verdict check, preventing replay.
success_criteria_results.evidence: commands/push.md Step 4 explicit abort instruction; rm -f grant unlink before verdict evaluation

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: grant is state-bound (nonce, branch, head_sha, remote_name, session_id) and single-use (unlinked after validation)
success_criteria_results.criterion: Push grant is state-bound and single-use
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read commands/push.md grant validation and consumption logic
success_criteria_results.details: commands/push.md Step 4 validates: nonce (matches REQUEST_ID), branch (matches BRANCH), head_sha (matches HEAD_SHA), remote_name (matches REMOTE_NAME), session_id (matches SESSION_ID), verdict enum (approved/warn/blocked only), risks type (JSON array), expires_at (future timestamp). Grant unlinked with rm -f immediately after validation before verdict check.
success_criteria_results.evidence: commands/push.md Step 4 grant validation block listing all 9 fields + rm -f unlink

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: merge-analyst dispatched BEFORE merge.sh; branch name passed from orchestrator (analyst does NOT infer); grant includes source_tip + default_tip
success_criteria_results.criterion: Merge-analyst dispatched before merge.sh, branch from orchestrator, grant includes tips
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read commands/merge.md step ordering and dispatch parameters
success_criteria_results.details: commands/merge.md Step 2 dispatches merge-analyst (with RESOLVED_BRANCH, SOURCE_TIP, DEFAULT_TIP, DEFAULT_BRANCH, REQUEST_ID, SESSION_ID, REPO_HASH), Step 5 calls merge.sh. Branch is resolved from conversation context in Step 0 and passed to analyst — analyst does not infer it.
success_criteria_results.evidence: commands/merge.md Step 2 before Step 5; dispatch parameters block explicitly listing RESOLVED_BRANCH as input

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: immediately before calling merge.sh, re-read branch tips; abort if changed
success_criteria_results.criterion: Branch tips revalidated immediately before merge.sh call
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read commands/merge.md Step 4 tip revalidation logic
success_criteria_results.details: commands/merge.md Step 4 re-reads CURRENT_SOURCE_TIP and CURRENT_DEFAULT_TIP via git rev-parse, compares to grant's source_tip/default_tip values. If either changed: abort with 'Branch tips changed since merge-analyst ran — re-run /merge to get a fresh analysis'.
success_criteria_results.evidence: commands/merge.md Step 4 tip revalidation block; abort message text

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: merge grant expires in 60 seconds; orchestrator re-dispatches (does not abort) if grant is expired
success_criteria_results.criterion: Merge grant expires in 60s; expired grant triggers re-dispatch not abort
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read agents/merge-analyst.md grant expiry and commands/merge.md expiry handling
success_criteria_results.details: agents/merge-analyst.md Phase 8 writes grant with expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(seconds=60). commands/merge.md Step 3 validates expires_at is in the future; on expiry: 're-dispatch merge-analyst (return to Step 2 with a fresh REQUEST_ID). Report to the user that the grant expired and a fresh analysis is running.'
success_criteria_results.evidence: agents/merge-analyst.md timedelta(seconds=60); commands/merge.md Step 3 re-dispatch-on-expiry instruction

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: pull_exit_phase uses presence of rebase-state-directory (.git/rebase-merge or .git/rebase-apply) as PRIMARY discriminant on non-zero exit
success_criteria_results.criterion: pull_exit_phase uses rebase-state-directory presence as primary discriminant; worktree-safe paths
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read commands/pull.md Step 4 exit phase logic
success_criteria_results.details: commands/pull.md Step 4 determines rebase state using: REBASE_MERGE=$(git rev-parse --git-path rebase-merge 2>/dev/null || echo '.git/rebase-merge') and REBASE_APPLY=$(git rev-parse --git-path rebase-apply 2>/dev/null || echo '.git/rebase-apply'). rebase_state_exists is set if either directory exists, and is the primary discriminant: if PULL_EXIT_CODE != 0 AND rebase_state_exists: pull_exit_phase='rebase_conflict' (no pull-analyst dispatch). pull-analyst dispatched only for 'success' and 'stash_restoration_failed'.
success_criteria_results.evidence: commands/pull.md Step 4 rebase state detection using git rev-parse --git-path; rebase_state_exists conditional before dispatch

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: pull-analyst is advisory-only (no grant written, no gate); orchestrator displays report without blocking
success_criteria_results.criterion: Pull-analyst is advisory-only, no grant, no gate
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read agents/pull-analyst.md output contract and commands/pull.md Step 6
success_criteria_results.details: agents/pull-analyst.md explicitly states: 'No grant token written' and 'No files written (Write tool not available) — all output goes to stdout only'. commands/pull.md Step 6: 'Display the pull-analyst advisory report to the user. This is informational only — no gate, no abort.' Policy confirms: pull-analyst allowed_tools=[Read,Glob,Grep,Bash] with Write excluded.
success_criteria_results.evidence: agents/pull-analyst.md advisory-only declarations; commands/pull.md Step 6 'no gate, no abort'; policy_registry.is_allowed('pull-analyst','Write',path) returns (False, 'tool Write not in allowed_tools')

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: each agent file has an explicit DO NOT section that forbids: git commit, push, merge, pull, rebase, reset --hard, branch -D, force operations, writing grants for other agents
success_criteria_results.criterion: All three agent files have explicit DO NOT sections forbidding git write ops and cross-agent grant writing
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read DO NOT sections in all three agent files; verify coverage of required prohibitions
success_criteria_results.details: push-analyst.md: 11-item DO NOT section. Items 1-8 explicitly name git push, commit, merge, pull, rebase, reset --hard, branch -D, force ops. Item 9: 'Never write grants for other agents'. PASS. merge-analyst.md: 12-item DO NOT section. Same git ops coverage. Item 9: 'Never write grants for other agents'. PASS. pull-analyst.md: 11-item DO NOT section. Same git ops coverage. Item 9: 'Never write any grant token' (not the exact phrase 'writing grants for other agents'). Items 9+10 together provide equivalent and actually stronger protection ('Never write any grant token' + 'Never write any file'). MINOR FINDING (non-blocking): AC9 literal phrase 'writing grants for other agents' is not verbatim present in pull-analyst.md item 9; the functional prohibition is equivalent.
success_criteria_results.evidence: agents/push-analyst.md DO NOT section items 1-11; agents/merge-analyst.md DO NOT section items 1-12; agents/pull-analyst.md DO NOT section items 1-11

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: hooks/push.sh, hooks/merge.sh, hooks/pull.sh are unchanged (zero diff vs HEAD) per architect constraint
success_criteria_results.verification_method: git diff HEAD -- hooks/push.sh hooks/merge.sh hooks/pull.sh
success_criteria_results.details: git diff HEAD for all three wrapper scripts produced zero output. hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py has a pre-existing working-tree modification from a prior session (last committed at 2203f42, before this task), confirmed not introduced by this task via git log. The modification is 2 small changes unrelated to push/merge/pull analyst design.
success_criteria_results.evidence: git diff HEAD -- hooks/push.sh hooks/merge.sh hooks/pull.sh: empty output; git log --oneline -1 hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py: 2203f42 (predates task)

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: policy_registry.is_allowed('push-analyst','Write','/tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/...') returns (True,'ok'); is_allowed('pull-analyst','Write',any_path) returns (False,...); all three in CP_AGENTS
success_criteria_results.criterion: Policy registration correct: push/merge analyst can write to their paths, pull-analyst Write hard-denied, all three in CP_AGENTS
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Live policy_registry.is_allowed() calls via Python; read hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py CP_AGENTS set
success_criteria_results.details: Verified live: policy_registry.is_allowed('push-analyst','Write','/tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/x/y/z.json') -> (True, 'ok'). policy_registry.is_allowed('push-analyst','Write','/tmp/other/x.json') -> (False, 'write target not in allowed_write_path_prefixes'). policy_registry.is_allowed('pull-analyst','Write','/tmp/agentic-commit/pull-analyst/x.json') -> (False, 'tool Write not in allowed_tools'). pull-analyst allowed_tools=[Read,Glob,Grep,Bash] (Write absent). hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py CP_AGENTS set contains 'push-analyst', 'merge-analyst', 'pull-analyst' at lines 72-73.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Live Python policy_registry.is_allowed() test results; hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py CP_AGENTS set lines 72-73

qa._success_criteria_rule: 11 AC items verified, 11 entries produced. All match.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The root cause was the absence of analyst subagents for /push, /merge, and /pull commands. All three now have dedicated analyst agent files with full agentic dispatch protocols integrated into their respective command bodies. The implementation mirrors changelog-analyst architecture with appropriate adaptations for each command's semantics.

build_verification.skip_reason: All changes are agent prompt files (.md) and configuration files (JSON, Python). No deployed web services, no compiled code, no containers affected. Changes take effect at agent invocation time, not build time.

regression_test_results.details: policies/tool-policy.v1.json confirmed valid JSON with 4 role entries. Live import via Python confirmed parse succeeds.
hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py confirmed parseable with ast.parse and contains all three new roles alongside existing 'changelog-analyst'.

regression_test_results.test: Wrapper scripts unchanged (no regression to hook behavior)
regression_test_results.details: hooks/push.sh, hooks/merge.sh, hooks/pull.sh show zero diff vs HEAD — no regression risk to wrapper logic.

e2e_enforcement.health_check_result: N/A — no web service affected by this change

ui_test_results.reason: No user-facing web UI changed. All modifications are agent prompt files (.md), configuration (JSON), and hook Python files. The agentic dispatch mechanism operates at Claude agent invocation time, not via a web browser interface.

all_findings.issue: AC9 specifies each agent must explicitly forbid 'writing grants for other agents'. pull-analyst.md item 9 says 'Never write any grant token' and item 10 says 'Never write any file — this agent has no Write tool access'. The functional prohibition is present (and actually stronger — it forbids all writes, not just cross-agent grants), but the exact AC9 phrase 'writing grants for other agents' does not appear verbatim.
all_findings.recommendation: In a future cycle, update pull-analyst.md DO NOT item 9 to explicitly mention 'writing grants for other agents' in addition to the existing stronger prohibition. Example: 'Never write any grant token (including grants for other agents) or any other file — Write tool is not available to this agent.' This aligns literal wording with AC9 while preserving the stronger policy-level protection.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex reviewed all implementation files (agents/push-analyst.md, agents/merge-analyst.md, agents/pull-analyst.md, commands/push.md, commands/merge.md, commands/pull.md, policies/tool-policy.v1.json, hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py) and the git diff. Codex confirmed: (1) refs/remotes range correct, no @{u} anywhere; (2) grant validation covers all required fields; (3) 60s merge expiry with re-dispatch correct; (4) rebase-state-directory discriminant correct; (5) pull-analyst advisory-only contract enforced at policy layer; (6) worktree-safe git rev-parse --git-path usage correct; (7) fork-prefer-origin remote resolution correct; (8) CP_AGENTS registration complete. Codex did not raise any additional blockers or majors beyond QA's own minor AC9 wording finding. Codex confirmed the implementation is production-ready.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: No changes needed. Codex confirmed the draft verdict of PASS with 1 minor finding. The minor finding (pull-analyst DO NOT item 9 wording) was independently identified by QA and corroborated by Codex's review. No Codex finding required verdict change.
